title: Centre for Regions, Trade & Geopolitics - Home
text: Centre for Regions, Trade and GeopoliticsCentre for Regions, Trade and GeopoliticsHelping stakeholders navigate geopolitical shifts and drive economic reforms. We engage governments, business, international organizations and civil society to lead initiatives, advance dialogue, and deliver insight amid a fast-changing global contextA shifting geopolitical landscape is introducing new layers of complexity and risk for business nationally, regionally, and globally.The Centre for Regions, Trade and Geopolitics helps stakeholders navigate these challenges and opportunities in the intelligent age, and provides them with a trusted platform to shape cooperation and drive economic reforms.190+Leading global companies40+International organizations150+Civil society organizationsOur initiativesOur initiatives bring together public and private sector leaders to address global and regional challenges through dialogue, cooperation and practical action.European Leaders for Growth and CompetitivenessA group focused on European growth and competitiveness made up of key leaders from public, private, civil society and expert organizations.Global Alliance for Trade FacilitationThis initiative supports governments in developing and least-developed countries in implementing the WTO’s Trade Facilitation Agreement.Humanitarian & Resilience Investing Initiative This initiative catalyzes private capital to unlock social impact investing in frontier market and increase the resilience of at-risk and crisis-hit communities.Global Data Partnership Against Forced LabourThis initiative aims to accelerate systems-level efforts to combat forced labour by developing technology tools to scale data sharing, while inspiring new approaches towards action.Our impactWe work with partners across sectors to respond to shifting geopolitical dynamics and support more open, stable and resilient economies.Removing Barriers to GrowthThe Global Alliance for Trade Facilitation is reshaping trade reform in developing and least-developed countries by simplifying cross-border trade, making it faster and more inclusive.TradeTech is revolutionizing global tradeThe TradeTech initiative encourages trade facilitation by improving transparency, fostering inter-agency cooperation and enabling data sharing for more effective risk management.Top organizations unlock investments to support the world’s most vulnerablePartners of the Humanitarian and Resilience Investing Initiative (HRI) announce over 50 commitments that will boost impact investment and could unlock over $15 billion.Boosting capital for developmentFor the past few years, the World Economic Forum has been piloting sustainable investment facilitation in Cambodia, Ghana, Kenya, and India to help tackle shortfalls in sustainable foreign direct investment (FDI).Join our work on regions, trade and geopoliticsWe believe multistakeholder dialogue and cooperation underpin the ability to achieve progress on shared priorities. The Centre partners with governments, business, civil society groups and international organizations to generate insight and deliver cooperative solutions to issues of global and regional concern. Be part of this work and join our community.World Economic Forum CentresThe world is confronted with a range of unprecedented challenges. In an increasingly complex global landscape, governments, international organizations, civil society groups and businesses need to work together to find common solutions and take decisive action.Through its centres, the Forum integrates public-private efforts to achieve greater impact.Centre for Regions, Trade and GeopoliticsPowered by© 2026 All rights reservedPrivacy Policy & Terms of Service
